bibliographic_data.title: Robust Long Range Magnetic Correlation across Anti-phase Domain Boundaries in Sr$_2$CrReO$_6$
bibliographic_data.abstract: Anti-site disorder is one of the most important issues that arises in synthesis of double perovskite for spintronic applications. Although it is known that anti-site disorder leads to a proliferation of structural defects, known as the anti-phase boundaries that separate ordered anti-phase domains in the sample, little is known about the magnetic correlation across these anti-phase boundaries on a microscopic level. Motivated by this, we report resonant elastic X-ray scattering study of room temperature magnetic and structural correlation in a thin-film sample of Sr2CrReO6, which has one of the highest TC among double perovskites. Structurally, we discovered existence of anti-phase nanodomains of ∼15 nm in the sample. Magnetically, the ordered moments are shown to lie perpendicular to the c direction. Most remarkably, we found that the magnetic correlation length far exceeds the size of individual anti-phase nanodomains. Our results therefore provide conclusive proof for existence of robust magnetic correlation across the anti-phase boundaries in Sr2CrReO6.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study provides evidence for robust long-range magnetic correlation across anti-phase boundaries in Sr2CrReO6, which far exceeds the size of individual anti-phase nanodomains.
core_content.key_methodology: Resonant elastic X-ray scattering (REXS) was used to investigate the room temperature magnetic and structural correlations in a thin-film sample of Sr2CrReO6. The technique allowed for the determination of the magnetic correlation length, which was found to be much longer than the average size of anti-phase nanodomains.
outcomes: Discovery of anti-phase nanodomains of ∼15 nm in the sample
Magnetic moments are perpendicular to the c direction
Magnetic correlation length far exceeds the size of individual anti-phase nanodomains
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this study, we investigated the magnetic and structural correlations in a thin-film sample of Sr2CrReO6 using resonant elastic X-ray scattering (REXS). The sample consists of small anti-phase nanodomains with an average size of ∼15 nm. The ordered moments are found to lie perpendicular to the c direction. Most remarkably, the magnetic correlation length is much longer than the size of individual anti-phase nanodomains, providing evidence for robust long-range magnetic correlation across the anti-phase boundaries in Sr2CrReO6.
